    Home › Manufacturers › Lockheed

    Lockheed

    United States · Founded 1912

    Military giants, one beautiful trijet, and the fastest aircraft ever built.

    Aircraft here2
    Longest75.31 m
    Combined built381
    First flights1968–1970
    The company

    The story so far

    Lockheed's reputation rests on doing what others could not: the U-2, the SR-71 Blackbird, and — in the heavy-lift world — the C-5 Galaxy, which for decades was the largest aircraft in the Western world and can still swallow loads nothing else in NATO can carry.

    Its one great airliner was the L-1011 TriStar: technically the most advanced jet of its decade, capable of automatic landings in 1972, and a commercial disaster that ended Lockheed's civil aircraft business for good. The company merged with Martin Marietta in 1995 to become Lockheed Martin.

    The line-up

    Lockheed aircraft

    Silhouettes are sized by real wingspan — bigger aircraft draw bigger.

    MTOW (-200)195,045 kg

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
